//: Example Finora rules feed. Host this JSON at a URL (e.g. a GitHub raw link) and paste that URL into Settings → Rules & Facts → Rule updates. Bump `version` whenever you add a rule. Sync is additive: only rules whose `kind` an install doesn't already have are inserted; existing rules are never overwritten. Each spec carries EITHER `sql` (a deterministic `D` rule) or `prompt` (an `L`/`L+` rule); the SQL runs read-only and must select the finding-draft columns (key, title, detail, value, confidence, evidence_summary, evidence_records, created_at, plus optional dollar_impact_minor, currency, urgency, effort, severity, action_label, account_id).

specs.sql: SELECT t.id AS key, 'Large ATM / cash withdrawal' AS title, money(ABS(t.amount_minor), t.currency) || ' withdrawn on ' || t.date AS detail, money(ABS(t.amount_minor), t.currency) AS value, ABS(t.amount_minor) AS dollar_impact_minor, t.currency AS currency, 0.4 AS confidence, 1 AS effort, 'low' AS severity, 'ATM/cash withdrawal of ' || money(ABS(t.amount_minor), t.currency) || ' on ' || t.date || '.' AS evidence_summary, t.id AS evidence_records, t.account_id AS account_id, t.created_at AS created_at FROM transactions t WHERE t.amount_minor < 0 AND julianday(:now_iso) - julianday(t.date) <= 30 AND ABS(t.amount_minor) >= 40000 AND (lower(t.description) LIKE '%atm%' OR lower(COALESCE(t.category, '')) LIKE '%cash%') ORDER BY ABS(t.amount_minor) DESC LIMIT 10
